note: Features UI is the optional admin front end for the Features module: the /admin/config/development/features screens for exporting, editing, diffing and downloading feature modules and configuring bundles. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented UI facts; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: After enabling the Features UI submodule on a Drupal 11 site, at what admin path do I manage and export feature packages?
evals.expected_output: Admin → Configuration → Development → Features, at /admin/config/development/features (route features.export, form FeaturesExportForm).

evals.prompt: I have the Features module but see no admin screens for it. How do I get the browser UI for packaging configuration?
evals.expected_output: Enable the features_ui submodule (drush en features_ui -y); it is the optional UI and depends on features:features. The base module ships no UI of its own.

evals.prompt: Which Features UI screen shows the difference between a site's active configuration and what a feature module stores, and what route serves it?
evals.expected_output: The Differences screen (route features.diff, form FeaturesDiffForm) at /admin/config/development/features/diff/{featurename}.

evals.prompt: In the Features admin UI, how do I add or remove individual configuration items from a feature package before exporting it?
evals.expected_output: Use the Edit screen (route features.edit, form FeaturesEditForm) at /admin/config/development/features/edit/{featurename}; the 'Create new feature' action link also routes there.

evals.prompt: Which route is the configure/settings entry point of the Features UI submodule for editing feature bundles?
evals.expected_output: features.assignment (Configure Bundles, AssignmentConfigureForm) at /admin/config/development/features/bundle/{bundle_name}; per-method forms (_alter, _base, _core, _exclude, _optional, _profile, _site) branch from it.

evals.prompt: Which permissions gate the Features UI screens? Does the submodule define its own permissions?
evals.expected_output: It defines no permissions of its own; it reuses core permissions — 'export configuration' for the main export/download screens and 'administer site configuration' for editing features and bundles.

evals.prompt: Can I get a feature module as a downloadable archive from the Features UI instead of writing it to disk?
evals.expected_output: Yes — the UI serves a tar archive via the base module's features.export_download route (/admin/config/development/features/download/{uri}), using the 'archive' generation method.
